content.full_text:
/ 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Paul carpenter 
Senator Paul Carpenter Committee 
P.O. Box 1210 
Norwalk, 
CA 
90651 
Dear Mr. Carpenter: 
May 5, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-112 
This is in response to your request for advice, and to confirm 
the advice I provided by telephone to Ann Peifer of your office, 
concerning the campaign disclosure obligations of the Senator Paul 
Carpenter Committee. 
Your letter lists several contributions which were made by the 
Committee in February, March and April to candidates involved in 
various local elections, as well as a $5,000 contribution to 
citizens Who Care, a committee primarily formed to support a 
candidate in the special state election for the 33rd Senate 
District. 
You have asked what campaign disclosure reports are 
required to be filed in connection with those contributions and 
when they are due. 
As I advised Ms. Peifer on April 21, 1987, in addition to its 
'semi-annual disclosure obligation, the Committee is required to 
fJle a Supplemental Pre-Election Statement (Form 495) pursuant to 
Government Code Section 84202.5 in connection with the $5,000 
contribution to citizens Who Care. 
The Form 495, along with a 
Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Long 
,Form/Consolidated Campaign Statement (Form 490) covering the 
period January 1, 1987 through April 25, 1987, must be filed no 
.> later than April 30, 1987, which is the filing deadline for the 
pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the May 12 
run-off election. 
Ms. P8ifer indicated that because the $5,000 
contribution was made the day before the March 17 primary 
election, a late contribution report had also been filed. 
She 
also stated that a late contribution report had been filed in 
connection with the April 3, 1987, loan of $4,000 made to Friends 
of John Greenwood in connection with his April 14 election. 
I advised Ms. Peifer that the filings discussed above are the 
only additional special reports required to be filed by the 
Committee for the first six months of 1987. 


I

Senator Paul Carpenter 
Page 2 
If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to 
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 14, 1987 
Paul Carpenter 
Senator Paul Carpenter Committee 
P.o. Box 1210 
Norwalk, CA 
90651 
Re: 
87-112 
Dear Mr. Carpenter: 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform 
Act was received on April 9, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If your request is for informal assistance, we will 
answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

sections.question: What campaign disclosure reports are required to be filed in connection with several contributions made by the Senator Paul Carpenter Committee to candidates and committees in February, March and April, including a $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care in connection with the special state election for the 33rd Senate District, and when are they due?
sections.conclusion: In addition to its semi-annual disclosure obligation, the Committee is required to file a Supplemental Pre-Election Statement (Form 495) pursuant to Government Code Section 84202.5 in connection with the $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care. The Form 495, along with a Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Long Form/Consolidated Campaign Statement (Form 490) covering the period January 1, 1987 through April 25, 1987, must be filed no later than April 30, 1987, which is the filing deadline for the pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the May 12 run-off election. The filings discussed are the only additional special reports required to be filed by the Committee for the first six months of 1987.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked what campaign disclosure reports must be filed by the Senator Paul Carpenter Committee for various contributions made in early 1987, particularly regarding a $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care for the 33rd Senate District special election, and what are the filing deadlines.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Committee must file a Form 495 (Supplemental Pre-Election Statement) and a Form 490 covering January 1 through April 25, 1987, both due by April 30, 1987, in connection with the $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care. These are the only additional special reports required for the first six months of 1987, as late contribution reports were already filed for the earlier contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains three component documents: initial request letter with contribution table (April 18, 1987), acknowledgment letter (April 14, 1987), and formal advice response (May 5, 1987). Extracted information from the primary advice letter (May 5, 1987) signed by General Counsel. Question and conclusion sections paraphrased from body text as no formal Q&C sections exist.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What campaign disclosure reports are required to be filed in connection with several contributions made by the Senator Paul Carpenter Committee to candidates and committees in February, March and April, including a $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care in connection with the special state election for the 33rd Senate District, and when are they due?

CONCLUSION: In addition to its semi-annual disclosure obligation, the Committee is required to file a Supplemental Pre-Election Statement (Form 495) pursuant to Government Code Section 84202.5 in connection with the $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care. The Form 495, along with a Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Long Form/Consolidated Campaign Statement (Form 490) covering the period January 1, 1987 through April 25, 1987, must be filed no later than April 30, 1987, which is the filing deadline for the pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the May 12 run-off election. The filings discussed are the only additional special reports required to be filed by the Committee for the first six months of 1987.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Diane M. Griffiths advises Senator Paul Carpenter's Committee regarding campaign disclosure obligations for contributions made in early 1987, confirming telephone advice that a Form 495 and Form 490 must be filed by April 30, 1987 in connection with a $5,000 contribution to Citizens Who Care for the 33rd Senate District special election.
